clusters.title: US and Iran trade threats to unleash 'hell' as search for missing US airman continues
clusters.neutral_headline: What we know so far about the US fighter jet shot down over Iran
bullet_summary: US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that Tehran had 48 hours left to cut a deal or face "all Hell", as US and Iranian forces scrambled to find a downed American airman
Reported by BBC World, CBS News, Financial Times and others

items.title: US and Iran trade threats to unleash 'hell' as search for missing US airman continues
items.standfirst: Donald Trump wants Iran to strike a deal and to open the strategic Strait of Hormuz whose closure has led to a spike in oil prices.
items.content: Donald Trump wants Iran to strike a deal and to open the strategic Strait of Hormuz whose closure has led to a spike in oil prices.

items.title: U.S., Iran racing to find missing U.S. airman after war plane shot down
items.standfirst: President Trump says Iran has 48 hours to make a deal before "all Hell will reign down on them." The U.S. military is also racing to find a missing American airman from one of two planes shot down by Iran on Friday. Elizabeth Palmer reports.
items.content: President Trump says Iran has 48 hours to make a deal before "all Hell will reign down on them." The U.S. military is also racing to find a missing American airman from one of two planes shot down by Iran on Friday. Elizabeth Palmer reports.

items.title: Trump makes fresh Iran threat as search continues for missing US airman
items.standfirst: US president threatens to rain ‘hell’ down if Strait of Hormuz does not reopen within 48 hours
items.content: US president threatens to rain ‘hell’ down if Strait of Hormuz does not reopen within 48 hours

items.title: Trump gives Iran 48 hours to make a deal as search continues for missing US pilot
items.standfirst: US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that Tehran had 48 hours left to cut a deal or face "all Hell", as US and Iranian forces scrambled to find a downed American airman.
items.content: US President Donald Trump said on Saturday that Tehran had 48 hours left to cut a deal or face "all Hell", as US and Iranian forces scrambled to find a downed American airman.

items.title: What we know so far about the US fighter jet shot down over Iran
items.standfirst: The pilot of the downed jet has been safely rescued, but a search and rescue operation is still under way for the jet's second crew member, US media reports.
items.content: The pilot of the downed jet has been safely rescued, but a search and rescue operation is still under way for the jet's second crew member, US media reports.

items.title: Trump says Iran has 48 hours to make deal as search for US pilot continues
items.standfirst: Trump does not reference a downed US jet in his post but points to a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.
items.content: Trump does not reference a downed US jet in his post but points to a deadline for Iran to reopen the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Search for pilot of downed U.S. fighter jet enters second day
items.standfirst: The search for the pilot of a U.S. fighter jet that went down over Iran entered its second day on Saturday, as Iranian officials denied reports that the American crew member has been detained.U.S.
items.content: The search for the pilot of a U.S. fighter jet that went down over Iran entered its second day on Saturday, as Iranian officials denied reports that the American crew member has been detained.U.S.

items.title: Search for missing US crew member of downed fighter jet enters second day
items.standfirst: Military pilots said F-15 crew member would be trying to hide for as long as possible from the Iranian militaryMiddle East crisis – live updatesUS search and rescue efforts for the missing second crew member of the downed F-15E fighter jet continued into a second day as Iran came under heavy bombing and Israel extended the war in Lebanon.A pilot had been rescued on Friday after the F-15E Strike Eagle became the first US plane to be downed over Iran during the five-week-long war, but the second of the two-strong crew has not been accounted for. Continue reading...
items.content: Military pilots said F-15 crew member would be trying to hide for as long as possible from the Iranian militaryMiddle East crisis – live updatesUS search and rescue efforts for the missing second crew member of the downed F-15E fighter jet continued into a second day as Iran came under heavy bombing and Israel extended the war in Lebanon.A pilot had been rescued on Friday after the F-15E Strike Eagle became the first US plane to be downed over Iran during the five-week-long war, but the second of the two-strong crew has not been accounted for. Continue reading...

items.title: 4/4: Saturday Morning

U.S. officials said one crew member was rescued after ejection, while the fate of the second crewmember remained unknown…

clusters.title: Iran conflict to forefront of UK religious and political leaders’ Easter messages
clusters.neutral_headline: Archbishop to pray for Middle East peace in first Easter sermon
bullet_summary: Reported by Guardian World, BBC Top Stories

items.title: Iran conflict to forefront of UK religious and political leaders’ Easter messages
items.standfirst: Archbishop of Canterbury to issue urgent call for peace, as PM exhorts Britons to ‘choose community over division’Religious and political leaders in the UK are highlighting the conflict in the Middle East in their Easter messages, calling for “peace, justice and freedom” in the region.The archbishop of Canterbury will deliver her first Easter sermon at Canterbury Cathedral on Sunday as the Church of England’s top bishop. Dame Sarah Mullally will call “with renewed urgency” for peace in the Middle East and pray for “an end to the violence and destruction” in the region. Continue reading...
items.content: Archbishop of Canterbury to issue urgent call for peace, as PM exhorts Britons to ‘choose community over division’Religious and political leaders in the UK are highlighting the conflict in the Middle East in their Easter messages, calling for “peace, justice and freedom” in the region.The archbishop of Canterbury will deliver her first Easter sermon at Canterbury Cathedral on Sunday as the Church of England’s top bishop. Dame Sarah Mullally will call “with renewed urgency” for peace in the Middle East and pray for “an end to the violence and destruction” in the region. Continue reading...

items.content: A Russian drone hit a busy spot in the southern Ukrainian town on Saturday morning, injuring another 21 people.

items.title: Russian drone strike on Ukrainian market kills five, Moscow intensifies attacks
items.standfirst: A Russian drone hit a covered market in the eastern Ukrainian city of Nikopol on Saturday, killing five people and injuring 30 others, according to Ukrainian officials. The Ukrainian air force said Russia fired 286 drones overnight, of which 260 were intercepted.
items.content: A Russian drone hit a covered market in the eastern Ukrainian city of Nikopol on Saturday, killing five people and injuring 30 others, according to Ukrainian officials. The Ukrainian air force said Russia fired 286 drones overnight, of which 260 were intercepted.

But regulators are increasingly concerned about what many clinics are selling and what claims they’re…

items.title: What are peptides, are they safe and is there evidence to back up the hype?
items.standfirst: Influencers and athletes are among those claiming substances can help with injury repair, weight loss and angi-ageingMedicines watchdog to investigate UK peptide clinics over health claims From influencers to athletes, high-profile figures are hailing peptides as the route to wellness, claiming they help with injury repair, weight loss, anti-ageing and mood. We take a look at what these substances are, and the murky industry surrounding them. Continue reading...
items.content: Influencers and athletes are among those claiming substances can help with injury repair, weight loss and angi-ageingMedicines watchdog to investigate UK peptide clinics over health claims From influencers to athletes, high-profile figures are hailing peptides as the route to wellness, claiming they help with injury repair, weight loss, anti-ageing and mood. We take a look at what these substances are, and the murky industry surrounding them. Continue reading...

items.title: Medicines watchdog to investigate UK peptide clinics over health claims
items.standfirst: Exclusive: Guardian investigation finds several clinics making potentially unlawful claims about benefits of unregulated therapiesWhat are peptides, are they safe and is there evidence to back up the hype?The medicines regulator is investigating whether UK clinics are breaking the law by making claims about the benefits of unregulated, experimental peptide therapies, the Guardian can reveal.Interest in experimental peptides has boomed in recent years. The substances are delivered by injection and are touted by sellers, influencers and even some medics as aiding everything from anti-ageing to recovery from injury. Continue reading...
items.content: Exclusive: Guardian investigation finds several clinics making potentially unlawful claims about benefits of unregulated therapiesWhat are peptides, are they safe and is there evidence to back up the hype?The medicines regulator is investigating whether UK clinics are breaking the law by making claims about the benefits of unregulated, experimental peptide therapies, the Guardian can reveal.Interest in experimental peptides has boomed in recent years. The substances are delivered by injection and are touted by sellers, influencers and even some medics as aiding everything from anti-ageing to recovery from injury. Continue reading...

items.standfirst: Roberto Mazzarella, head of a notorious Camorra clan, had been on the run for more than a yearAn Italian mafia boss, who was one of Italy’s most dangerous fugitives, has been arrested on murder charges after more than a year on the run, Italian police said on Saturday.Roberto Mazzarella was the head of the notorious Mazzarella clan of the Camorra – the Naples-based organised crime gang. Continue reading...
